Container.Name: Eyeris
Container.Overview: Self-hosted photo manager for NAS and local libraries. Eyeris scans your photos, generates thumbnails, detects NSFW content, and gives you a fast searchable gallery — all on your own hardware. AI-powered image descriptions and tagging are offloaded to a connected A-Eye instance; Eyeris imports the results automatically. No cloud, no subscriptions, no local LLM required. Features include: fast GPU-accelerated NSFW detection and tag classification, A-Eye integration for AI descriptions and tags, EXIF and GPS metadata, duplicate and blurry photo detection, album organisation, SearXNG web image search, and a real-time dashboard.
Container.Requires: Optional but recommended: a running A-Eye instance for AI image descriptions and tagging. Optional: MariaDB for large libraries (SQLite used by default).

Config.Name: Web UI Port
Config.Description: Port for the Eyeris web interface and API.

Config.Name: Photo Directory
Config.Description: Your photo library — local folder or SMB/NFS mount point.

Config.Name: Thumbnails
Config.Description: Where Eyeris stores generated thumbnails and face crop cache.

Config.Name: Database
Config.Description: SQLite database storage. Ignored if DB_HOST is set.

Config.Name: Config file (.env)
Config.Description: Persistent config file — Eyeris reads and writes settings here. Create an empty file at this path before first run.

Config.Name: Intel / AMD GPU device
Config.Description: Pass through Intel iGPU/Arc or AMD GPU for hardware-accelerated thumbnail generation. Remove this line if using NVIDIA only.

Config.Name: NAS Host
Config.Description: IP or hostname of your NAS. Leave empty if photos are already accessible via the Photo Directory path above.

Config.Name: NAS Username
Config.Description: SMB/CIFS username for your NAS.

Config.Name: NAS Password
Config.Description: SMB/CIFS password for your NAS.

Config.Name: NAS Shares
Config.Description: Comma-separated share names to scan, e.g. photos,media,camera-uploads

Config.Name: A-Eye URL
Config.Description: URL of your A-Eye instance, e.g. http://192.168.1.100:7171. Connect A-Eye to enable AI image descriptions and automatic tagging.

Config.Name: A-Eye Username
Config.Description: Username for HTTP Basic Auth on your A-Eye instance. Leave empty if A-Eye is unauthenticated.

Config.Name: A-Eye Password
Config.Description: Password for HTTP Basic Auth on your A-Eye instance.

Config.Name: SearXNG URL
Config.Description: URL of a SearXNG instance for the web image search feature (optional).

Config.Name: Scan Workers
Config.Description: Number of parallel scan workers. Raise to 3-4 on fast systems; lower if your system struggles.

Config.Name: Scan Interval (minutes)
Config.Description: How often Eyeris rescans for new photos.

Config.Name: Scan Schedule Enabled
Config.Description: Set to true to restrict scanning to a time window (e.g. overnight only).

Config.Name: Scan Schedule Start
Config.Description: Start of the allowed scan window (24-hour HH:MM). Only used when SCAN_SCHEDULE_ENABLED=true.

Config.Name: Scan Schedule End
Config.Description: End of the allowed scan window (24-hour HH:MM). An end time before the start time crosses midnight.

Config.Name: MariaDB Host
Config.Description: MariaDB host IP or hostname. Leave empty to use the built-in SQLite database (recommended for most users).

Config.Name: MariaDB Port
Config.Description: MariaDB port.

Config.Name: MariaDB User
Config.Description: MariaDB username.

Config.Name: MariaDB Password
Config.Description: MariaDB password.

Config.Name: MariaDB Database
Config.Description: MariaDB database name.
